summary_en: Ordinary dividend raised 53→59 yen/share (+6 yen); commemorative dividend 10 yen; total period-end 69 yen. FY3/26. Policy: DOE 2%+ floor, payout ratio 30%+.
title_en: Notice of Variance Between Full-Year Earnings Forecast and Actual Results and Revision (Increase) of Dividend Forecast for FY3/26
translation_en_brief:
On 15 May 2026, Kikusui Holdings announced an upward revision to its dividend forecast for the fiscal year ending 31 March 2026. The company raised its ordinary dividend from 53 yen per share to 59 yen per share (an increase of 6 yen). Combined with a commemorative dividend of 10 yen per share, the total period-end dividend is now 69 yen per share.

The revision follows better-than-expected earnings performance. Full-year net income attributable to parent shareholders reached 1,609 million yen, up 11.0% from the prior forecast of 1,450 million yen, driven by strong demand in automotive-related, energy-related, semiconductor-related, and data-center-related equipment investment markets.

The company's dividend policy targets a Dividend on Equity (DOE) floor of 2% or more, with a consolidated payout ratio of 30% or higher as the stable base for shareholder returns.
